resources.string: Ship small Android apps from a local workspace
Add an OpenAI, DeepSeek, or compatible API key before creating a project. The agent needs the model to turn requirements into source code.
Configure a model API key first.
Enter a bootstrap zip URL first.
Delete %1$s and all local messages, source, logs and APK files?
Describe the app or the next change
adb shell pm grant com.androidbuilder com.termux.permission.RUN_COMMAND
Termux is not installed. Install Termux first, open it once, then return here.
Grant the Termux run-command permission, then tap setup again.
Termux command permission is already granted.
Permission denied. Open this app permissions and enable “Run commands in Termux environment”.
Enable “Display over other apps” for Termux, then return and tap setup again.

Termux setup:

There are two different permissions:

1. Android Builder needs “Run commands in Termux environment”. This belongs to Android Builder, not Termux. Tap “Request Termux command permission” if you cannot find it in system settings. On some OPPO/ColorOS devices this custom permission is hidden; use the ADB permission command fallback.

2. Termux needs “Display over other apps” so it can start terminal sessions from the background.

Termux itself cannot be silently installed on normal Android. First install Termux, open it once, then enable external commands:
 mkdir -p ~/.termux
 echo allow-external-apps=true >> ~/.termux/termux.properties
Restart Termux.

After that, tap Run Termux Setup. The Copy buttons are fallback options.

Android Builder includes an embedded runtime integration point designed for GPLv3-compatible Termux components.

Termux app: GPLv3-only. Source: https://github.com/termux/termux-app
Termux packages: https://github.com/termux/termux-packages
terminal-emulator and terminal-view notices must be preserved if their code is bundled.

If this APK is distributed with Termux GPLv3 code or binaries, distribute the corresponding source code, license text, copyright notices, and any local modifications under GPLv3 terms.

The embedded backend creates a private runtime layout under files/runtime. Bootstrap zip layout may contain usr/bin, usr/android-sdk, home, or direct bin/android-sdk entries; they are installed into the private runtime.
